name: Logrosán and Sierra Brava Reservoir Loop
name_original: Desde Logrosán hasta el Embalse de Sierra Brava — circular en BTT
description: This moderate 44.8-mile mountain biking loop from Logrosán to the Sierra Brava Reservoir offers varied landscapes and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Logrosán are set within the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara UNESCO Global Geopark, characterized by an "Appalachian-type" relief with parallel mountains and valleys. The region features diverse geological formations, including ancient synclinal and anticlinal valleys, providing varied terrain for cycling. Extensive holm oak forests and Mediterranean woods offer shaded paths, while numerous rivers and reservoirs add to the scenic beauty. This landscape provides a dynamic environment for mountain biking, from moderate trails to challenging climbs. Best mountain bike trails around Logrosán The most popular mountain bike trail is Church of Cañamero – Pico Villuercas Summit loop from Cañamero, a difficult 52.2 miles (84.1 km) trail that takes 8 hours 9 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain towards Pico Villuercas Summit. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Logrosán and Sierra Brava Reservoir Loop, a moderate 44.8 miles (72.1 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and views of the Sierra Brava Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Cerro Cabrero loop from Logrosán, a 26.1 miles (42.0 km) trail leading through the rugged landscape around Cerro Cabrero, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Logrosán is defined by its "Appalachian-type" relief, parallel mountains and valleys, and extensive holm oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Logrosán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Logrosán?
A: There are 8 mountain bike trails around Logrosán, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes are set within the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara UNESCO Global Geopark, known for its unique geological formations and diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Logrosán?
A: The terrain around Logrosán is characterized by an "Appalachian-type" relief, featuring a striking succession of parallel mountains and valleys. You can expect varied surfaces, including extensive holm oak forests, Mediterranean woods, and some unpaved or difficult segments. Routes often follow natural paths, including sections of the Camino Natural de Las Villuercas, which incorporates old railway lines and more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Logrosán?
A: While there are no trails specifically rated as 'easy' in this selection, many of the 7 moderate trails offer varied terrain that can be enjoyed by riders with some experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206185754/logrosan-and-sierra-brava-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logrosán and Sierra Brava Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 44.8-mile route with varied terrain and views of the Sierra Brava Reservoir, which might be a good starting point for those looking for a less strenuous ride compared to the difficult options.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in the Logrosán area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Cañamero – Pico Villuercas Summit loop from Cañamero&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.2-mile trail with significant elevation gain towards Pico Villuercas Summit. This route offers panoramic views from the highest point in the Geopark.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Logrosán pass through a region rich in natural beauty and geological heritage. You can encounter diverse ecosystems, holm oak forests, and Mediterranean woods. Notable features include the dramatic "sea of cliffs and valleys" visible from viewpoints, and water bodies like the Cíjara reservoir dam. Some routes may also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6042271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho del Fresno Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;. The wider Geopark is also home to the impressive Cueva de Castañar de Ibor, a Natural Monument.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Logrosán?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Logrosán are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Cabrero loop from Logrosán&lt;/a&gt; is a 26.1-mile trail that takes you through the rugged landscape around Cerro Cabrero. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer – Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer loop from Navalvillar de Pela&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 41.7-mile route.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Logrosán?
A: The Logrosán region, part of Extremadura, generally experiences hot summers. Spring and autumn are typically the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Winter can also be suitable, though some higher elevation areas might be cooler. It's always advisable to check local weather conditions before planning your ride.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical or cultural sites?
A: Yes, many trails in the Logrosán area integrate cultural and historical elements. Routes often pass through traditional villages like Cañamero, allowing cyclists to explore local architecture and access natural springs. The wider region is also close to the UNESCO World Heritage site of the Royal Monastery of Guadalupe, adding a cultural dimension to your biking excursions. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5324617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isabel la Católica Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails?
A: Logrosán and nearby villages within the Geopark offer various services for visitors. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in these towns. Many trails pass through or near villages, providing opportunities to replenish supplies and experience local hospitality.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Logrosán?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Logrosán varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana de la Sierra Village – Orellana de la Sierra loop from Navalvillar de Pela&lt;/a&gt;, covering 27.7 miles, typically takes around 4 hours to complete. More challenging and longer routes, like the difficult 52.2-mile &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Cañamero – Pico Villuercas Summit loop from Cañamero&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 8 hours.

Q: Is there public transport available for cyclists in Logrosán?
A: While specific public transport options for cyclists directly to trailheads might be limited, Logrosán is accessible by regional bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycle transport in advance if you plan to use public transport to reach the area or specific starting points for your rides.

Q: What are the parking facilities like for mountain bikers in Logrosán?
A: Logrosán and the surrounding villages typically offer public parking areas. When planning your ride, it's recommended to identify suitable parking near your chosen trailhead, especially for longer routes. Many routes start from or pass through towns where parking is generally available.
tips: On a wonderfully navigable trail, the Vía Verde takes you consistently flat and completely off-road through a vast landscape of wetlands and agricultural areas. The small and old railway viaducts through which streams and rivers cross are especially beautiful.
In this barren landscape, a beautiful blue lake is a feast for the eyes. Whoever would like to jump in and leave some of the daytime heat in it.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It requires a good level of physical fitness and some experience with mountain biking. You can expect natural obstacles like roots, stones, and tighter turns, typical of an STS S2 level trail.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the circular route from Logrosán to Sierra Brava Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, covering approximately 72.1 kilometers. You'll encounter a mix of landscapes, including sections that are quite flat, areas with considerable vegetation, and paths running between stone fences. There are also parts that pass through scattered oak and eucalyptus groves, possibly following old railway lines, interspersed with more technical sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Sierra Brava Reservoir, which is the destination of this circular route. You will also ride along sections of the Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana, offering scenic views of the region's natural beauty, including wildflowers and diverse tree species.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this mountain bike route in Logrosán?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Logrosán, you can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride. Specific parking areas for trail access are usually well-marked or available near the town center, allowing convenient access to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in sections where wildlife might be present. Please ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Logrosán region experiences hot summers, so the best times to ride this trail are typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery, including wildflowers in spring, is at its most vibrant.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route from Logrosán to the Sierra Brava Reservoir. It is a public access trail.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the Vía Verde de las Vegas del Guadiana y las Villuercas, and also crosses parts of the Camino Romano - Etapa 5 and Camnino Visigodo - Etapa 4.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect a journey through a region rich in natural beauty, including areas with abundant wildflowers, scattered oak and eucalyptus groves, and varied landscapes leading to the impressive Sierra Brava Reservoir. The area boasts significant scenic, geological, ecological, and historical-cultural richness.

faqs.question: Are there any restaurants or places to eat in Logrosán for cyclists?
faqs.answer: Logrosán, being the starting and ending point of this circular route, offers various local establishments where cyclists can find refreshments and meals. You'll find traditional Spanish cuisine and places to refuel after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner mountain bikers looking for an easier option?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, this route is not ideal for absolute beginners. While some sections might be less challenging, the overall length (72.1 km) and technical aspects (STS S2) require a good level of fitness and some prior mountain biking experience. Beginners might prefer shorter, flatter routes in the region.
